iti94:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti94:2.1:
“Monks, a monk should investigate in such a way that– his consciousness neither externally scattered & diffused,
nor internally positioned–he is, from lack of clinging/sustenance, unagitated,
iti94:2.2: and there is no coming into being of the origination of future birth, aging, death, or stress.”
iti94:3.1: For a monk who has abandoned seven attachments
iti94:3.3: the wandering-on in birth is finished;
iti94:3.4: there is, for him, no further becoming.
